title: podman image scp: added identity for ssh.Exec
body:
Added forgotten "Identity" parameter to ssh.Exec

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
```
Fix a bug in image-scp to correctly use identity settings.
```

Thank you for your PR! CI is failing because we require regression tests - to make sure future PRs don't lose this functionality. Can you find a way to add a test for this? If not, see the CI-failure message for instructions on how to proceed.

> Thank you for your PR! CI is failing because we require regression tests - to make sure future PRs don't lose this functionality. Can you find a way to add a test for this? If not, see the CI-failure message for instructions on how to proceed.

I don't know how to make automated tests for this PR. What should I do in this case?

I don't know enough about `image scp` to offer suggestions. I would start by asking, what problem does this PR try to solve? What behavior is broken today, and how can we make sure that this PR really fixes it? Is there a specific command that fails when you run it today, and works with your PR? Then see if that could be written as a test in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/e2e/image_scp_test.go

...but ssh stuff is tricksy, it could be that the setup is too complicated to automate. If so, see the `Validate` test failure log for instructions on how to bypass the new-test check.

Sorry for delay.

I wanted to copy container images from one host to another using the podman SCP command.

According to the "podman-system-connection-add" man page, it has option "--identity=path":
```
Path to ssh identity file. If the identity file has been encrypted, Podman prompts the user for the 
passphrase.  If no identity file is provided and no user is given, Podman defaults to the user running the 
podman command.  Podman prompts for the login password on the remote server. 
```

Firstly, I added new connection via "podman system connection add" command with "—identity=private_key" option specified.
```
podman system connection list
Name   URI                                                                                             Identity
LOCAL  ssh://user12@127.0.0.1:22/run/podman/podman.sock    /usr/home/user12/.ssh/test_id_rsa
```

After that, I tried to copy container image from one host to another via "podman image scp" command.
Received the error. 
```
podman image scp quay.io/dougrabson/hello LOCAL:hello2
Copying blob c0e5c0479511 done
Copying config f81c971736 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none], no supported methods remain
```


Then I found the missprint in SCP command code, that "Identity" field is empty in "ssh.ConnectionExecOptions{}" struct while trying to execute ssh.Exec() (see the "Files changed" in PR). After changes was made, I compiled the new binary with my fixes. As a result, image copying works correctly.
```
podman image scp quay.io/dougrabson/hello LOCAL:hello2
Copying blob c0e5c0479511 done
Copying config f81c971736 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image: quay.io/dougrabson/hello:latest
```

Thank you! We don't have any way to test `podman system connection`, so please:
1. edit the comment-zero field of this PR (the one copied from your commit message) to include the string `[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]`
2. rebase
3. repush

/approve

LGTM. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. I'm thinking this actually needs a release-note entry.
